title: buildah ignores the owner when copying files
body:
**Description**

In our environment we build scratch containers through a multi stage build. the first step is to build a chroot environment with the app and dependencies and set file permissions. the second step is to copy the chroot environment from the first step and set up user, port, volumes, etc. when we built our images via docker, the file permissions were inherited. buildah ignores the permissions and sets the current user as owner.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Build the following Dockerfile :

```
cat << EOF > Dockerfile
FROM ubuntu:20.04

RUN set -ex; \
        touch /test; \
        chown nobody /test

FROM scratch

COPY --from=0 /test /test
EOF

buildah bud --format docker -t copy-test .
```

2. Check the file permissions for /test.

**Describe the results you received:**

the file owner is root

**Describe the results you expected:**

the file owner is nobody

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.15.0
Go Version:      go1.14.4
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  v0.7.2-0.20190904153231-83439463f784
image Version:   5.5.1
Git Commit:      368c41b
Built:           Wed Jun 24 11:13:35 2020
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="20.04 LTS (Focal Fossa)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 20.04 LTS"
VERSION_ID="20.04"
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
VERSION_CODENAME=focal
UBUNTU_CODENAME=focal
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux 240c6d10005c 4.12.14-197.45-default #1 SMP Thu Jun 4 11:06:04 UTC 2020 (2b6c749) x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
chroot isolation
```
